Comment: Dispatches a single S3 Batch Operations job (S3PutObjectCopy) to seed a destination bucket with the FULL current contents of the source bucket, including objects that are themselves replicas (which S3ReplicateObject/Batch Replication skips via EligibleForReplication). Copy ignores replication status entirely, so it is the correct tool to backfill a bucket that was itself populated by inbound replication (e.g. a legacy source being decommissioned). Uses S3JobManifestGenerator (no S3 Inventory precondition). When CreatedAfter is provided the manifest is time-filtered so re-runs only copy newer objects (incremental catch-up); otherwise every current object is copied. The target object inherits the destination bucket's default SSE-KMS encryption, so no per-object key is set here. Native SDK only (no Lambda). createJob assumes the source role via Credentials.RoleArn.$, passes AccountId explicitly, supplies a fresh ClientRequestToken via States.UUID() with ConfirmationRequired:false (idempotency), and runs the copy under BatchCopyRoleArn.

HasCreatedAfter.Comment: Branch on the optional CreatedAfter time filter. Present -> incremental copy (only objects created after the cutoff). Absent -> full copy of every current object.

CreateCopyJobFull.Comment: Full copy: manifest generator lists every current object in the source bucket (no Filter). S3PutObjectCopy to the destination bucket; MetadataDirective COPY preserves source metadata; target encryption comes from the destination bucket's default SSE-KMS. SourceBucket and TargetResource are bucket ARNs (Pitfall: not names).

CreateCopyJobIncremental.Comment: Incremental copy: identical to CreateCopyJobFull but the manifest generator Filter restricts to objects created after CreatedAfter (ISO-8601), so a re-run only copies newer objects. Live replication covers new objects continuously; this is a batch catch-up lever.

PrepareOutput.Comment: Normalize success output with JobId at the top level so the orchestrator reads $.S3BatchResult.Output.JobId and check_batch_replication consumes $.BatchJob.JobId (same envelope as run_batch_replication).

CopyJobFailed.Cause: Failed to create the S3 Batch Copy job (s3control:createJob). See $.Error for the underlying error.
